problem: The client requires one durable, authenticated and serviceable delivery-address book that remains authoritative across cart, checkout and application restarts without local address truth or duplicate active records.
goal: Persist, read, update, default and soft-delete client-owned delivery addresses through DSH, with idempotency, optimistic concurrency, governed service-area validation, privacy controls and committed readback.

actors.scope: governed diagnostics and privacy actions only
persistence, validation, events and checkout resolution

excludedSurfaces.reason: Partners do not own client delivery-address books.
Field staff do not create or mutate client-owned addresses.
Captains receive order-scoped delivery snapshots only after order creation.
No authenticated client address-book runtime is registered on the public website.

acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated clients can list only their active addresses.
Create is idempotent across retries and application restarts.
An active logical duplicate is rejected with ADDRESS_ALREADY_EXISTS.
Update and delete reject stale versions with ADDRESS_CONFLICT.
Exactly one active address is default whenever active addresses exist.
Deleting the default deterministically promotes one remaining active address.
Checkout resolves a client-owned address by deliveryAddressId and never accepts a client-supplied snapshot as truth.
Coordinates and serviceAreaCode are verified by DSH before persistence.
Soft-deleted address PII follows the governed retention and anonymization policy.
The client refreshes the committed address list after every successful mutation.
No surface persists address records in AsyncStorage, localStorage, sessionStorage, MMKV, fixtures or mocks.
negativeInvariants: No clientId query parameter may select an address owner.
No cross-client read, update, default or delete is permitted.
No second active default or active logical duplicate is permitted.
No unverified or mismatched service-area coordinates may be committed.
No mutation success may be displayed before canonical response and readback.
No address PII may be emitted in logs, metrics labels or correlation identifiers.
decisionPolicy: READY_FOR_REVIEW requires all FS-01..FS-18 implementation checks on one commit. CLOSED_WITH_EVIDENCE additionally requires independent product, QA, security, privacy, device and release evidence.
